name: nmap-parser
summary:
nmap-parser
Wraps around nmap commands and parses results into a csv file, developed for MS17-010 exploit (WannaCry)

documentation.readme:
nmap-parser
Wraps around nmap commands and parses results into a csv file, developed for MS17-010 exploit (WannaCry)

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with bormaxi8080/nmap-scanner (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with Crazyfellow5900/Nmap-Service-And-Exploits-Detection (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with vsnbsabbineni/-Exploiting-Cross-Site-Scripting-XSS-Vulnerabilities-in-HTML-Context---BurpSuite (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web', 'cloud'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web', 'cloud'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 5: same attack_surface=['web', 'cloud'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use this tool when the engagement is in the 'any' phase against a target with '['web', 'cloud']' as the attack surface (web_application_security, toolbox, github).
documentation.why_to_use: This tool is preferred because: Index entry in the Web Application Security category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: Index entry in the Web Application Security category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.
Pairs with the Web Application Security system-prompt stanza in core.ai_backend.chain to teach the LLM the standard invocation pattern.
Operator-initiated: the chain step is gated by the per-step ACCEPT/CANCEL gate; the tool does not auto-execute.
Use as a reference implementation when designing a new v3 method for this category.
Use as a teaching example in the prompt stanza so the LLM can reason about the right run order.
